'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 高天彦神社 |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}北窪158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高天彦神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}
{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫命}}
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公 |神体 = 白雲岳（{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}） |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 彦沢権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月5日|Q2930}}{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=高天彦神社|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''高天彦神社'''（たかまひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}北窪にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 * {{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすびのかみ） - 主神。 * {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫命}}（いちきしまひめのみこと） * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神は1座。元々は当地の地主神の「高天彦」を祀ったものと推測される{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 社名・神名の「高天（たかま）」は一帯の地名でもあり、神話に見える{{jalink|高天原|Q1141974}}の伝承地とする説が古くからあるほか、高皇産霊神の神名の転訛が由来とする説、高皇産霊神の別名が「高天彦神」とする説、「高間」すなわち金剛山中腹の平地を意味するとする説がある{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。『{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}』では、「葛城の高間」と詠まれた歌が知られる（巻7 1337番）{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。『延喜式』神名帳では{{jalink|宇智郡|Q11449432}}に高天岸野神社・高天山佐太雄神社が見え、いずれも{{jalink|五條市|Q602571}}の金剛山中腹の神社に比定されることから、「高天彦神」を金剛山の神霊そのものとする説もある{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|白雲岳（{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}）}}{{small|画像左下に高天彦神社境内。}}]] 創建は不詳。{{jalink|金剛山 (金剛山地)|Q1348310|金剛山}}東麓に鎮座し、元々は社殿後背の白雲岳（白雲峰、標高985メートル）を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}に祀った神社とされる境内説明板。{{Sfn|高天彦神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒には「高天彦神 四戸{{sub|大和国　宝亀十年奉充}}」として、{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}10年（{{jalink|779年|Q31124}}）に大和国内から充てられた{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}の存在が記されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）に正四位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}にある「高天彦神」が吉野大后（{{jalink|井上内親王|Q7244649}}）の願いで四時幣帛に預かったと見えるが、内親王と当社の関係は明らかでない{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。その後{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}6年（{{jalink|839年|Q23874}}）には従三位の「高天彦神」が名神に列したこと、また{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）には従二位勲二等に昇叙された旨が記されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}に「高天彦神社 名神大 月次相嘗新嘗」として、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|相嘗祭|Q11581944}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}に際しては幣帛に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 その後の変遷は不詳。高天の地は金剛山への登山口であるため、葛城修験道の発展とともに文人・俳人が高天を訪れたと伝えるほか、当社は「彦沢権現」とも称されたという{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。また神社東側には、{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の高天寺があった{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}では{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列している{{Sfn|高天彦神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。現在の本殿は{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}10年（{{jalink|1877年|Q7773}}）に竣工したものである。竣工当時は{{jalink|茅葺き}}屋根だったが、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}52年（{{jalink|1977年|Q2481}}）に{{jalink|石州瓦|Q11586446}}の屋根に葺き替えられ、更に{{jalink|令和|Q62609017}}元年({{jalink|2019年|Q25274}})には{{jalink|ガルバリウム鋼板}}の屋根に葺き替えられた。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）{{jalink|4月26日|Q2532}}、正四位上 （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 * {{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}6年（{{jalink|839年|Q23874}}）{{jalink|5月26日|Q2586}}、従三位、名神に列す （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、正三位勲二等から従二位勲二等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 地方自治体史 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=奈良県史 5 神社|publisher=名著出版|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} * 百科事典 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=高天彦神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 30 奈良県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|高天彦神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=高天彦神社|title={{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 29 奈良県|publisher={{jalink|角川書店|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|高天彦神社（角川）|1990年}}}} * その他書籍 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=池田末則|chapter=高天彦神社|year=1982|title=式内社調査報告 第2巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編|author=木村芳一|year=1985|chapter=高天彦神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 4 大和|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫命}}
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公 |神体 = 白雲岳（{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}） |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 彦沢権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月5日|Q2930}}{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''高天彦神社'''（たかまひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}北窪にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 * {{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}（たかみむすびのかみ） - 主神。 * {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫命}}（いちきしまひめのみこと） * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神は1座。元々は当地の地主神の「高天彦」を祀ったものと推測される{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 社名・神名の「高天（たかま）」は一帯の地名でもあり、神話に見える{{jalink|高天原|Q1141974}}の伝承地とする説が古くからあるほか、高皇産霊神の神名の転訛が由来とする説、高皇産霊神の別名が「高天彦神」とする説、「高間」すなわち金剛山中腹の平地を意味するとする説がある{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。『{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}』では、「葛城の高間」と詠まれた歌が知られる（巻7 1337番）{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。『延喜式』神名帳では{{jalink|宇智郡|Q11449432}}に高天岸野神社・高天山佐太雄神社が見え、いずれも{{jalink|五條市|Q602571}}の金剛山中腹の神社に比定されることから、「高天彦神」を金剛山の神霊そのものとする説もある{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|白雲岳（{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}）}}{{small|画像左下に高天彦神社境内。}}]] 創建は不詳。{{jalink|金剛山 (金剛山地)|Q1348310|金剛山}}東麓に鎮座し、元々は社殿後背の白雲岳（白雲峰、標高985メートル）を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}に祀った神社とされる境内説明板。{{Sfn|高天彦神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒には「高天彦神 四戸{{sub|大和国　宝亀十年奉充}}」として、{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}10年（{{jalink|779年|Q31124}}）に大和国内から充てられた{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}の存在が記されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）に正四位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}にある「高天彦神」が吉野大后（{{jalink|井上内親王|Q7244649}}）の願いで四時幣帛に預かったと見えるが、内親王と当社の関係は明らかでない{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。その後{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}6年（{{jalink|839年|Q23874}}）には従三位の「高天彦神」が名神に列したこと、また{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）には従二位勲二等に昇叙された旨が記されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}に「高天彦神社 名神大 月次相嘗新嘗」として、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|相嘗祭|Q11581944}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}に際しては幣帛に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 その後の変遷は不詳。高天の地は金剛山への登山口であるため、葛城修験道の発展とともに文人・俳人が高天を訪れたと伝えるほか、当社は「彦沢権現」とも称されたという{{Sfn|高天彦神社（式内社）|1982年}}。また神社東側には、{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の高天寺があった{{Sfn|高天彦神社（神々）|1985年}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}では{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列している{{Sfn|高天彦神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。現在の本殿は{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}10年（{{jalink|1877年|Q7773}}）に竣工したものである。竣工当時は{{jalink|茅葺き}}屋根だったが、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}52年（{{jalink|1977年|Q2481}}）に{{jalink|石州瓦|Q11586446}}の屋根に葺き替えられ、更に{{jalink|令和|Q62609017}}元年({{jalink|2019年|Q25274}})には{{jalink|ガルバリウム鋼板}}の屋根に葺き替えられた。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）{{jalink|4月26日|Q2532}}、正四位上 （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 * {{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}6年（{{jalink|839年|Q23874}}）{{jalink|5月26日|Q2586}}、従三位、名神に列す （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、正三位勲二等から従二位勲二等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「高天彦神」。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 地方自治体史 ** {{Cite [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}})
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%BF%E8%A1%8C/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%BD%A6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Takamahiko Shrine] - Nara Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20201 Takamahiko Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}})
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}})
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%BF%E8%A1%8C/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%BD%A6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Takamahiko Shrine] - Nara Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20201 Takamahiko Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%BF%E8%A1%8C/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%BD%A6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Takamahiko Shrine] - Nara Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20201 Takamahiko Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}})
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}})
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 5|Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{jalink|Gose|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{jalink|Gojō|Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{jalink|Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in ''{{jalink|Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{jalink|Hōki|Q18520676}} 10 ({{jalink|779|Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{jalink|Princess Inoe|Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame-sai|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{jalink|1977|Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{jalink|Sekishū tiles|Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{jalink|2019|Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) {{jalink|April 26|Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 6 ({{jalink|839|Q23874}}) {{jalink|May 26|Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{jalink|Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Takamagahara|Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%BF%E8%A1%8C/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%BD%A6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Takamahiko Shrine] - Nara Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20201 Takamahiko Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名たかまひこしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社たかまひこしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289}}) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|WD=Q2930}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kitakubo, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{ill|lt=Gojō|Q602571|WD=Q602571}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{ill|lt=Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mt. Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] -
