Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|WD=Q2930|1=October 5}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kitakubo, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{ill|lt=Gojō|Q602571|WD=Q602571|1=Gojō, Nara}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{ill|lt=Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mt. Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124|1=779}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446|1=Sekishū-gawara}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532|1=April 26}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586|1=May 26}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - Gose Tourism Association * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%BF%E8%A1%8C/%E9%AB%98%E5%A4%A9%E5%BD%A6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Takamahiko Shrine] - Nara Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=20201 Takamahiko Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]]

[[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|WD=Q2930|1=October 5}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kitakubo, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{ill|lt=Gojō|Q602571|WD=Q602571|1=Gojō, Nara}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{ill|lt=Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mt. Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124|1=779}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446|1=Sekishū-gawara}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532|1=April 26}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586|1=May 26}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - {'text': '奈良県御所市北窪158', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|WD=Q2930|1=October 5}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kitakubo, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{ill|lt=Gojō|Q602571|WD=Q602571|1=Gojō, Nara}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{ill|lt=Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mt. Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124|1=779}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446|1=Sekishū-gawara}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532|1=April 26}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586|1=May 26}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] - {'text': '奈良県御所市北窪158', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県御所市高天', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:高天彦神社]] [[ZH:高天彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q650253}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takamahiko-Jinja-2023.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}Kitakubo 158 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|5|N|135|41|45.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takamahiko Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Hikosawa Gongen |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|WD=Q2930|1=October 5}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Takamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takamahiko Shrine''' (たかまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kitakubo, {{ill|lt=Gose|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are as follows{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) - principal deity. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the deity is listed as one seat. It is inferred that originally it enshrined the local land deity "Takamahiko"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. The name "Takama" in the shrine/deity name is also a local place name. Since old times there has been a theory identifying this area with the legendary {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}}. Other theories say the name derives from a phonetic change of Takamimusubi's name, or that Takamimusubi had an alternate name "Takamahiko-no-Kami," or that "Takama" means "high flatland" on the mid-slope of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}, a well-known poem refers to "Takama of Katsuragi" (Book 7, poem 1337){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. In the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, Takama Kishino Shrine and Takamayama Satao Shrine are listed in Uchi District and are both identified with shrines on Mt. Kongō's mid-slope in {{ill|lt=Gojō|Q602571|WD=Q602571|1=Gojō, Nara}}, which has led to a theory that "Takamahiko-no-Kami" itself is the mountain spirit of Mt. Kongō{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Takamahiko-jinja Shirakumo-dake.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shirakumo-dake ({{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}})}}{{small|Takamahiko Shrine precinct at lower left.}}]] Foundation is unknown. The shrine stands on the east foot of {{ill|lt=Mt. Kongō (Kongō Range)|Q1348310|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mt. Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and is said originally to have worshipped Shirakumo-dake (also Shirakumo peak, elevation 985 m) behind the shrine buildings as the sacred mountainExplanatory board in precincts.{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the record for {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in ''{{ill|lt=Shinshō Gekichokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'', "Takamahiko deity, four households (in Yamato province, allocated in Hōki 10)" is recorded, indicating shrine households allocated from within Yamato in {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 10 ({{ill|lt=779|Q31124|WD=Q31124|1=779}}){{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the Six National Histories, in {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), "Takamahiko deity" at Senior Fourth Rank Upper is said to have received regular seasonal offerings by wish of Princess Yoshino Daikō ({{ill|lt=Princess Inoe|Q7244649|WD=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}}), though the relation between the princess and this shrine is unclear{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}), it is recorded that "Takamahiko deity" at Junior Third Rank entered the myojin register; in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was promoted to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Grade{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in Katsuragi-kami District of Yamato as "Takamahiko Shrine, myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly, aidame, and niiname rites," and is recorded as receiving court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{ill|lt=Ainame-sai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. Subsequent history is unclear. Because Takama is a trailhead to Mt. Kongō, it is said that literati and haiku poets visited along with the growth of Katsuragi Shugendō, and the shrine was also called "Hikosawa Gongen"{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. On the east side of the shrine there was also Takama-dera, a {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The current honden was completed in Meiji 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}). At completion it had a thatched roof; in Shōwa 52 ({{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}}) it was re-roofed with {{ill|lt=Sekishū tiles|Q11586446|WD=Q11586446|1=Sekishū-gawara}}, and in Reiwa 1 ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}}) again re-roofed with galvalume steel panels. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) {{ill|lt=April 26|Q2532|WD=Q2532|1=April 26}}, Senior Fourth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=839|Q23874|WD=Q23874|1=839}}) {{ill|lt=May 26|Q2586|WD=Q2586|1=May 26}}, Junior Third Rank, entered myojin register (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Senior Third Rank Second Merit to Junior Second Rank Second Merit (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Takamahiko deity." === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Explanatory board in precincts * Local histories ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=History of Nara Prefecture 5: Shrines|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|History of Nara Prefecture 5|1989}}}} * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary of Japan|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 29 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}|isbn=978-4040012902|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1990}}}} * Other books ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Ikeda Suenori|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Kimura Yoshikazu|year=1985|chapter=Takamahiko Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites 4 Yamato|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|Takamahiko Shrine (Gods)|1985}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takamagahara|Q1141974|WD=Q1141974|1=Takamagahara}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Takamahiko-jinja}} * [http://goseshikankou.jp/syousai/otera-zinzya/katuraginomiti/takamahiko/takamahiko.html Takamahiko Shrine] -
